tier: H6 / H-f: HFS+ attribute overflow records (kHFSPlusAttrExtents) + broad attribute growth (multi-leaf attributes B-tree)
host: Host pre-check: Apple diskdev_cmds 540.1 fsck_hfs/newfs_hfs (built in WSL). The S.A.K. HFS+ writer CLI (sak_hfs_writer_cli.exe) produced the attributes; fsck_hfs validated. macOS-kernel mount + native-Apple fsck_hfs (hfs-683.100.9) + kernel xattr read-back is the certifying confirmation.
change: Implemented the H-f driver core: a STREAMING multi-leaf attributes B-tree engine mirroring the certified catalog (H2) and extents-overflow (H4) engines (load whole tree -> apply insert/remove edits -> split/merge leaves collapsing depth -> rebuild index levels bottom-up -> compose header), replacing the old single-leaf gate. (1) BROAD GROWTH: create/delete of inline + fork-backed attributes now grows the attributes B-tree to arbitrary depth/width (node-pool growth doubles each retry like the catalog). (2) ATTRIBUTE OVERFLOW RECORDS: a fork-backed attribute with more than 8 fragments emits a kHFSPlusAttrForkData record (startBlock 0, theFork.totalBlocks = ALL blocks, first 8 extents) plus one kHFSPlusAttrExtents (0x30) overflow record per additional group of up to 8 extents, each keyed by the cumulative startBlock -- mirroring the catalog file extents-overflow accounting. Records sort by (fileID, attrName, startBlock) per Apple's CompareAttributeKeys. The fragmenting allocator threads an arbitrary fragment count through the inline 8 + the overflow records; delete frees every extent across the fork-data and overflow records and collapses the tree. Layout/rules from Apple hfs_format.h + the fsck_hfs CheckAttributeRecord / AttrBTChk checks: forkData.totalBlocks must equal the sum of every extent (CheckLastAttrAllocation), each overflow record's key startBlock must equal the cumulative blocks seen so far, and overflow records must follow a forkData/overflow record for the same attribute.
scenario: MULTI-LEAF: newfs_hfs 32 MiB H6ATTR; 160 inline attributes on the root folder -> a depth-2 attributes B-tree (3 leaves under an index root, nodeSize 8192). OVERFLOW: newfs_hfs -b 512 8 MiB OVF2 fragmented (50 two-block files, every other deleted -> non-adjacent holes); a fork attribute (20000-byte value, 40 allocation blocks) is forced across the scattered holes into a fork-data record + 2 kHFSPlusAttrExtents overflow records; then deleted (frees all 40 blocks).

evidence_fsck.host_result: '** Checking extended attributes file.' + '** The volume ... appears to be OK.' on the 160-attribute depth-2 volume; same clean on the overflow fork-attribute volume after create, and after deleting the overflow attribute (40 blocks released).
evidence_fsck.negative_control_note: the same streaming load/edit/rebuild engine certified for H2/H4; deleting a non-existent attribute is rejected ('not found').

evidence_unit: tests/unit/test_partition_manager_core.cpp: hfsFileSystemWriter_growsAttributesNodePoolOnRootLeafSplit (50 inline creates all succeed -> attributes tree depth >= 2 / leafRecords >= 50; every attribute reads back across the multi-leaf fLink chain; consistency clean; delete every other -> consistency clean; survivor reads back). Full local core suite green (143/143); clang-format / lizard (CCN<=10, params<=5) / cppcheck (exhaustive) all clean; the obsolete single-leaf bounded engine was removed.
kernel_mount: CERTIFIED 2026-06-27 in a macOS Sequoia VM (qemu/KVM recovery, hfs kernel). MULTI-LEAF: the S.A.K.-written H6ATTR (bare HFS+ = /dev/disk0) was MOUNTED by the kernel at /Volumes/H6ATTR; 'xattr /Volumes/H6ATTR | wc -l' = 160 (the kernel enumerated all 160 attributes from the depth-2 multi-leaf attributes B-tree) and 'xattr -p user.attr099 | wc -c' = 221 (read a 220-byte value back); then Apple's NATIVE fsck_hfs (version hfs-683.100.9) on /dev/rdisk0: '** Checking extended attributes file. ... ** The volume H6ATTR appears to be OK.' OVERFLOW: the kernel mounted OVF2, 'xattr -p bigfork /Volumes/OVF2 | wc -c' = 20001 (the kernel reassembled the full 20000-byte value across the fork-data + kHFSPlusAttrExtents overflow records) and Apple native fsck_hfs (hfs-683.100.9): '** Checking extended attributes file. ... ** The volume OVF2 appears to be OK.' Evidence hfs.h6-kernel-mount-160-xattrs.png + hfs.h6-multileaf-apple-fsck-PASS.png + hfs.h6-overflow-kernel-read-fsck-PASS.png. The H6 lane flips to Yes.
